macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto market selloff deepens; Bitcoin, Ethereum, BNB, XRP and Solana extend losses
items.summary: Bitcoin extended its June slide to about $63,207, with reported long liquidations of $1.41 billion and US spot Bitcoin ETFs seeing another $396.60 million in daily outflows. Ethereum fell below $1,800, while BNB, XRP and Solana also declined sharply.

items.title: Bitcoin and major altcoins post steep 24-hour declines
items.summary: A market roundup reported Bitcoin near $60,000, Ethereum below $1,600, BNB around $574, XRP near $1.11 and Solana near $65, highlighting broad-based weakness across large-cap crypto assets. The move was described as a roughly 21% drop in 24 hours for some tokens in the roundup context.

items.title: Kraken opens institutional services to Tempo's stablecoin ecosystem
items.summary: Kraken announced institutional services support for Tempo, connecting Tempo’s stablecoin-focused blockchain with Kraken’s trading, custody, compliance and settlement infrastructure. The move expands infrastructure support for stablecoin use cases.

items.title: Standard Chartered keeps $100,000 Bitcoin target despite market drop
items.summary: Standard Chartered reportedly maintained its $100,000 Bitcoin target even after a recent 12% decline, with the bank characterizing the week as painful but possibly near downside exhaustion. The call reflects continued bullish longer-term sentiment despite short-term volatility.

items.title: Better and Coinbase launch Bitcoin-backed Fannie Mae mortgage loan product
items.summary: Better and Coinbase unveiled a mortgage product that lets qualified homebuyers use Bitcoin as collateral instead of selling their holdings. The product was presented as a new digital-asset use case in housing finance.

items.title: Arthur Hayes exits Zcash position after Orchard Pool exploit concerns
items.summary: Arthur Hayes said he sold his entire Zcash position, citing concern over an Orchard Pool exploit and arguing privacy coins need absolute mathematical certainty in the AI era. The announcement drew attention to security and privacy debates around ZEC.
